title: Memory Hierarchy and Cache Introduction
summary: Introduces the memory hierarchy (registers → cache → main memory → disk), explains the motivation for cache memory, and connects it to locality laws.
content_points: Processor is faster than main memory → must wait for data.
Hierarchy: each level is smaller and faster than the one below.
Cache: small, fast SRAM circuit between CPU and main memory.
Locality laws justify keeping recently used data in cache.
Cache lookup: check cache first; on miss, fetch from main memory.
learning_objectives: Explain why cache memory improves performance
Connect locality laws to cache behaviour
